title: Hydrologic Technicians
description: Collect and organize data concerning the distribution and circulation of ground and surface water, and data on its physical, chemical, and biological properties. Measure and report on flow rates and ground water levels, maintain field equipment, collect water samples, install and collect sampling equipment, and process samples for shipment to testing laboratories. May collect data on behalf of hydrologists, engineers, developers, government agencies, or agriculture.

tasks.text: Analyze ecological data about the impact of pollution, erosion, floods, and other environmental problems on bodies of water.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Answer technical questions from hydrologists, policymakers, or other customers developing water conservation plans.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Apply research findings to minimize the environmental impacts of pollution, waterborne diseases, erosion, or sedimentation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assist in designing programs to ensure the proper sealing of abandoned wells.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Collect water and soil samples to test for physical, chemical, or biological properties, such as pH, oxygen level, temperature, and pollution.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop computer models for hydrologic predictions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Estimate the costs and benefits of municipal projects, such as hydroelectric power plants, irrigation systems, and wastewater treatment facilities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Investigate complaints or conflicts related to the alteration of public waters by gathering information, recommending alternatives, or preparing legal documents.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Investigate the properties, origins, or activities of glaciers, ice, snow, or permafrost.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Locate and deliver information or data as requested by customers, such as contractors, government entities, and members of the public.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Measure the properties of bodies of water, such as water levels, volume, and flow.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform quality control checks on data to be used by hydrologists.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare, install, maintain, or repair equipment used for hydrologic study, such as water level recorders, stream flow gauges, and water analyzers.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Provide real time data to emergency management and weather service personnel during flood events.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Write groundwater contamination reports on known, suspected, or potential hazardous waste sites.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Write materials for research publications, such as maps, tables, and reports, to disseminate findings.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
